name: TB-500 (Ac-LKKTETQ)
subtitle: Thymosin Beta-4 Active Fragment | Healing Peptide

molecular.halfLife: Not precisely established; effects persist longer than 24 hours

overview: TB-500 (Ac-LKKTETQ) is a synthetic derivative of thymosin beta-4 consisting of the N-terminal acetylated 17-23 amino acid fragment. This sequence represents the active site within thymosin beta-4 responsible for actin binding, cell migration, and wound healing. Research shows it promotes endothelial cell differentiation, angiogenesis, keratinocyte migration, collagen deposition, and decreases inflammation. The acetylation protects against N-terminal degradation while maintaining biological activity.
mechanism: TB-500 contains the LKKTETQ sequence which is the actin-binding motif of full-length thymosin beta-4. This fragment shares many properties of the parent protein regarding cell proliferation, differentiation, and migration. It promotes angiogenesis by upregulating VEGF expression and enhancing endothelial cell sprouting. The peptide undergoes serial cleavage at the C-terminus during metabolism, while the N-terminal acetylation provides protection from degradation.

items.name: Wound Healing
items.description: Promotes dermal wound healing through the active LKKTETQ sequence.

items.name: Tissue Regeneration
items.description: Supports tissue repair comparable to full thymosin beta-4 in research.

items.name: Skin Repair
items.description: Promotes keratinocyte migration and collagen deposition.

items.name: Angiogenesis
items.description: Promotes endothelial cell differentiation and new blood vessel formation.

items.name: Inflammation Reduction
items.description: Decreases inflammatory responses in damaged tissues.

deliveryMethods.overview: TB-500 is typically administered via subcutaneous injection. The N-terminal acetylation provides stability and protection from degradation. Not authorized for medicinal use; research peptide only.

steps: Clean work area and hands thoroughly

interactions.notes: Commonly combined for enhanced healing effects through different mechanisms.
TB-500 is the active fragment; combining may be redundant.
Both support wound healing and tissue repair through different pathways.
Different mechanisms; may complement for tissue growth.

timeline.effects: Full healing benefits; transition to maintenance

references.title: Synthesis and Characterization of N-terminal Acetylated 17-23 Fragment of Thymosin Beta-4
references.keyFindings: TB-500 contains Ac-LKKTETQ, the active site responsible for actin binding and wound healing.

references.title: Doping Control Analysis of TB-500
references.keyFindings: TB-500 is a synthetic version of the active region of thymosin beta-4.

references.title: TB-500 Metabolism and Wound Healing
references.keyFindings: TB-500 undergoes serial cleavage at C-terminus; acetylation protects N-terminus.

references.title: Investigation of TB-500 Metabolism
references.keyFindings: Characterized human metabolism of TB-500 using liver microsomes and S9 fraction.
